theme: Allocate a sampling budget across domain regions with wildly heterogeneous hidden integrand variance.
title: Variance-Adaptive Quadrature Budget: Pilot-Probe, Then Neyman-Allocate
strategies: trivial: spend zero extra budget, rely on the pilot batch alone (reproduces the grader baseline (~0.1))

greedy: spread the extra budget evenly across all regions, ignoring both width and the pilot's noise signal -> on the spiked-variance traps it wastes budget on calm regions and starves the narrow, loud one

strong: estimate each region's noise from its pilot sample's standard deviation, floor the estimate against a small pilot's own sampling noise (a stopping-rule hedge), then run Neyman allocation -- budget proportional to width times the floored noise estimate -- concentrating the budget on the regions whose width-times-uncertainty product is largest
alternatives: allocate purely by region width (still blind to noise), or a fuller iterative water-filling refinement over marginal variance reduction per unit budget
